discussion_comments.comment_body: I wonder if this should be added to `autofix.yml` either instead or in addition to here. Autofix will commit the fixes to the branch.

discussion_comments.commented_code:
@@ -35,6 +35,8 @@ jobs:
           main-branch-name: main
       - name: Run Checks
         run: pnpm run test:pr --parallel=3
+      - name: Verify Links
discussion_comments.comment_body: Since this `verify-links.ts` only detects broken links but does not automatically fix them, I think it's more appropriate to keep it in `ci.yml` rather than `autofix.yml`. But please feel free to share if you had something else in mind

-    name: 'Test & Publish'
discussion_comments.comment_body: but wouldn't a fork also have those branches?

discussion_comments.commented_code:
@@ -23,14 +19,13 @@ env:
 
 jobs:
   test-and-publish:
-    if: github.repository == 'TanStack/query' && (github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' || github.ref == 'refs/heads/alpha' || github.ref == 'refs/heads/beta' || github.ref == 'refs/heads/rc')
-    name: 'Test & Publish'
discussion_comments.comment_body:
The following checks don't exist in the workflows on `main` anymore.

Even if they did, when you fork a repo the workflows are separate. So in the following code snippet:
```
on:
  push:
    branches: ['main', 'alpha', 'beta', 'rc', 'v4']
```
The workflow gets triggered only when a commit is pushed on the `main` `alpha` `beta` `rc` or `v4` branches. The workflows are separate when you fork a repo and a commit on a forked repo wouldn't trigger the workflow on the original repo.
